rationale: The actual resolution cites two things: duplicate of #4808 AND refer to #4809 for details; X addresses only the former and never mentions #4809, while Y explicitly reconstructs the #4809 triage (5 call sites, 4 fixed in #4810, this one scoped to #4808), directly engaging the evidence trail the maintainer pointed to. X also confidently states #4808 'is already merged on main' in its answer body while appending a disclaimer that merge-state was unverified — an internal contradiction — whereas Y flags the same uncertainty transparently ('#4808 is CLOSED and mergedAt is null') and reasons from the reporter's own repro on main @ 86bdcaf3d to argue the fix may not have landed, which is a more defensible, better-calibrated read given the available evidence. Both cite plausible file/line locations, but Y's grounding is reinforced by tying back to the #4809 reference actually present in the ground truth.
